sn43.11:1.1: “And which is the path leading to the unfabricated?
sn43.11:1.3: This is called the path leading to the unfabricated.
sn43.11:1.4: Thus, monks, I have taught you the unfabricated and the path leading to the unfabricated.
sn43.11:1.5: Whatever a sympathetic teacher should do—seeking the welfare of his disciples, out of sympathy for them—that have I done for you.
sn43.11:1.6: Over there are the roots of trees; over there, empty dwellings. Practice jhāna, monks. Don’t be heedless. Don’t later fall into remorse. This is our message to you all.”
